events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Scenario: Grounding electrode system, GEC spliced improperly, rough-complete. Need decision fail because non-compliant. Risk high? GEC splice improper can compromise grounding, safety critical. component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 80? value not asked 0. findings: GEC splice not made with irreversible compression connector or exothermic welding; splice not accessible; etc. actions: remove improper splice, install listed irreversible compression or exothermic splice, accessible location, re-inspect. references NEC 250.64(C)(1) maybe? Actually GEC must be continuous or spliced with irreversible compression connector or exothermic welding, accessible. NEC 250.64(C) grounding electrode conductor installation; (C)(1) splices. Also 250.68(C) maybe. Need include NEC 250.50-250.68. Let's craft JSON.

  "findings": [
    "Grounding electrode conductor (GEC) splice is improper",
    "Splice does not use an irreversible compression connector or exothermic welding",
    "Splice location may not be accessible for inspection",
    "Improper splice compromises the integrity of the grounding electrode system"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the improper GEC splice",
    "Install a listed irreversible compression connector or make an exothermic weld splice",
    "Ensure the splice is accessible and suitable for inspection",
    "Re-inspect and test grounding electrode conductor continuity before close-in"
  ],
  "rationale": "The GEC is a critical safety component of the grounding electrode system. NEC requires it to be continuous or spliced only with irreversible compression connectors or exothermic welding, and splices must be accessible. An improper splice is a code violation and creates a shock/fire hazard, so the installation fails rough inspection.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 250.64(C)(1) - Grounding electrode conductor splices",
    "NEC 250.64(C) - Grounding electrode conductor installation",
    "NEC 250.50-250.68 - Grounding and bonding electrode system requirements"
  ]
}
